question: Will I be more happy than sad after reading the comments?
textDescription:
For the purpose of this market, "happy" refers to any positive emotion, and "sad" refers to any negative emotion. I will not bet on this market; those who know me personally must not bet on this market.

I will read each comment at least twice - once after it is posted, and once again before market resolution. If the comment is hidden, I won't re-read it before resolution. Comments must not violate Manifold's terms of service or community guidelines, and must not have shocking or disturbing embeds; comments violating any of those rules will be hidden and reported if necessary.
Market resolves according to my own opinion on my feelings after reading the comments.

Some potentially relevant information:
1. I'm autistic
2. I'm less prone to anger than most people

Feel free to ask more in the comments. I will only answer questions which I feel comfortable answering; if I'm not comfortable answering a question, please do not push it.

Update 2026-05-22 (PST) (AI summary of creator comment): Obscene or lewd comments (e.g. sexual/horny content) will be disallowed per Manifold ToS and hidden/reported. Such comments will not be re-read before resolution.
newsShortReason: Given your autism and lower anger propensity, and strict comment rules, positive emotions are more likely than negative ones.
newsKeyEvidence: The creator's autism and lower tendency to anger suggest emotional responses may skew positive or neutral. | Comments are regulated to avoid offensive or disturbing content, reducing likelihood of negative emotions. | The requirement to re-read comments twice implies a thoughtful engagement, potentially softening negative impact. | Comments violating terms of service will be hidden, limiting exposure to distressing content.
